blocks.description: This is a very loose definition, in the future, we'll populate this with deeper schema in this definition namespace.
blocks.title: Block Kit blocks

defs_topic_purpose_creator.title: User ID or empty string, used for topic and purpose creation

unlinked.title: Field to determine whether a channel has ever been shared/disconnected in the past
Field to determine whether a channel has ever been shared/disconnected in the past

items.title: Conversation IM Channel Object from conversations.* methods
Nil bot_id set when display_as_bot is false

info.description: One way to interact with the Slack platform is its HTTP RPC-based Web API, a collection of methods requiring OAuth 2.0-based user, bot, or workspace tokens blessed with related OAuth scopes.
info.title: Slack Web API

post.description: Sends a message to a channel.

parameters.description: Authentication token. Requires scope: `chat:write`
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Pass true to post the message as the authed user, instead of as a bot. Defaults to false. See [authorship](#authorship) below.
parameters.name: as_user

parameters.description: A JSON-based array of structured attachments, presented as a URL-encoded string.
parameters.name: attachments

parameters.description: A JSON-based array of structured blocks, presented as a URL-encoded string.
parameters.name: blocks

parameters.description: Channel, private group, or IM channel to send message to. Can be an encoded ID, or a name. See [below](#channels) for more details.
parameters.name: channel

parameters.description: Emoji to use as the icon for this message. Overrides `icon_url`. Must be used in conjunction with `as_user` set to `false`, otherwise ignored. See [authorship](#authorship) below.
parameters.name: icon_emoji

parameters.description: URL to an image to use as the icon for this message. Must be used in conjunction with `as_user` set to false, otherwise ignored. See [authorship](#authorship) below.
parameters.name: icon_url

parameters.description: Find and link channel names and usernames.
parameters.name: link_names

parameters.description: Disable Slack markup parsing by setting to `false`. Enabled by default.
parameters.name: mrkdwn

parameters.description: Change how messages are treated. Defaults to `none`. See [below](#formatting).
parameters.name: parse

parameters.description: Used in conjunction with `thread_ts` and indicates whether reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation. Defaults to `false`.
parameters.name: reply_broadcast

parameters.description: How this field works and whether it is required depends on other fields you use in your API call. [See below](#text_usage) for more detail.
parameters.name: text

parameters.description: Provide another message's `ts` value to make this message a reply. Avoid using a reply's `ts` value; use its parent instead.
parameters.name: thread_ts

parameters.description: Pass true to enable unfurling of primarily text-based content.
parameters.name: unfurl_links

parameters.description: Pass false to disable unfurling of media content.
parameters.name: unfurl_media

parameters.description: Set your bot's user name. Must be used in conjunction with `as_user` set to false, otherwise ignored. See [authorship](#authorship) below.
parameters.name: username

schema.description: Schema for successful response of chat.postMessage method
schema.title: chat.postMessage success schema

default.description: Typical error response if too many attachments are included

schema.description: Schema for error response chat.postMessage method

callstack.description: Note: PHP callstack is only visible in dev/qa

schema.title: chat.postMessage error schema

get.description: Fetches a conversation's history of messages and events.

parameters.description: Authentication token. Requires scope: `conversations:history`
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Conversation ID to fetch history for.
parameters.name: channel

parameters.description: End of time range of messages to include in results.
parameters.name: latest

parameters.description: Start of time range of messages to include in results.
parameters.name: oldest

parameters.description: Include messages with latest or oldest timestamp in results only when either timestamp is specified.
parameters.name: inclusive

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return. Fewer than the requested number of items may be returned, even if the end of the users list hasn't been reached.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Paginate through collections of data by setting the `cursor` parameter to a `next_cursor` attribute returned by a previous request's `response_metadata`. Default value fetches the first "page" of the collection. See [pagination](/docs/pagination) for more detail.
parameters.name: cursor

200.description: Typical success response containing a channel's messages

messages.text: I find you punny and would like to smell your nose letter
What, you want to smell my shoes better?

schema.description: Schema for successful response from conversations.history method
schema.title: conversations.history success schema

schema.description: Schema for error response from conversations.history method

callstack.description: Note: PHP callstack is only visible in dev/qa

schema.title: conversations.history error schema

get.description: Lists all channels in a Slack team.

parameters.description: Authentication token. Requires scope: `conversations:read`
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Set to `true` to exclude archived channels from the list
parameters.name: exclude_archived

parameters.description: Mix and match channel types by providing a comma-separated list of any combination of `public_channel`, `private_channel`, `mpim`, `im`
parameters.name: types

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return. Fewer than the requested number of items may be returned, even if the end of the list hasn't been reached. Must be an integer no larger than 1000.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Paginate through collections of data by setting the `cursor` parameter to a `next_cursor` attribute returned by a previous request's `response_metadata`. Default value fetches the first "page" of the collection. See [pagination](/docs/pagination) for more detail.
parameters.name: cursor

200.description: Typical success response with only public channels

purpose.value: This channel is for team-wide communication and announcements. All team members are in this channel.
A place for non-work-related flimflam, faffing, hodge-podge or jibber-jabber you'd prefer to keep out of more focused work-related channels.

topic.value: Non-work banter and water cooler conversation

schema.title: conversations.list success schema

schema.description: Schema for error response from conversations.list method

callstack.description: Note: PHP callstack is only visible in dev/qa

schema.title: conversations.list error schema

get.description: Retrieve a thread of messages posted to a conversation

parameters.description: Authentication token. Requires scope: `conversations:history`
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Conversation ID to fetch thread from.
parameters.name: channel

parameters.description: Unique identifier of a thread's parent message. `ts` must be the timestamp of an existing message with 0 or more replies. If there are no replies then just the single message referenced by `ts` will return - it is just an ordinary, unthreaded message.
parameters.name: ts

parameters.description: End of time range of messages to include in results.
parameters.name: latest

parameters.description: Start of time range of messages to include in results.
parameters.name: oldest

parameters.description: Include messages with latest or oldest timestamp in results only when either timestamp is specified.
parameters.name: inclusive

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return. Fewer than the requested number of items may be returned, even if the end of the users list hasn't been reached.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Paginate through collections of data by setting the `cursor` parameter to a `next_cursor` attribute returned by a previous request's `response_metadata`. Default value fetches the first "page" of the collection. See [pagination](/docs/pagination) for more detail.
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Searches for messages matching a query.

parameters.description: Authentication token. Requires scope: `search:read`
parameters.name: token

parameters.description: Pass the number of results you want per "page". Maximum of `100`.
parameters.name: count

parameters.description: Pass a value of `true` to enable query highlight markers (see below).
parameters.name: highlight

parameters.description: Search query.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Return matches sorted by either `score` or `timestamp`.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Change sort direction to ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`).
parameters.name: sort_dir

matches.text: The meaning of life the universe and everything is 42.
The meaning of life the universe and everything is 101010

application/json.query: The meaning of life the universe and everything

schema.description: This method either only returns a brief _OK_ response or a verbose schema is not available for this method.
schema.title: Default success template

schema.description: This method either only returns a brief _not OK_ response or a verbose schema is not available for this method.
schema.title: Default error template
